'''Ian Sibbit''' (born 15 October 1980), also known by the nickname of '''"Sibs"''', is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a second row.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

Sibbit signed for Salford in 2004 from the [[Warrington Wolves]] having also played for [[Melbourne Storm]]. He also went on to play for [[Bradford Bulls]].

==Bradford Bulls==

He signed a two-year deal with the [[Bradford Bulls]] for 2011's [[Super League XVI]]. He is not seen as a direct replacement for [[Steve Menzies]] but is seen as a back-up player. Statistics do NOT include pre-season friendlies.

'''2011''' – [[2011 Bradford Bulls season|2011 Season]]

Sibbit appeared in two of the four pre-season games. He played against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

Ian featured in Round 1 and 2 against [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Wigan Warriors]]. His next game would be in Round 4 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) the in Round 5 ([[Huddersfield Giants]]). Sibbit then appeared in Round 9 ([[Hull F.C.]]). Ian featured in three consecutive games from Round 12 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 14 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]). He appeared in another three consecutive game between Round 17 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]) and Round 19 ([[Leeds Rhinos]]). Sibbit then featured in Round 24 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) until Round 26 ([[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] games against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Wigan Warriors]]. He scored a try against Halifax.

'''2012''' – [[2012 Bradford Bulls season|2012 Season]]

Sibbit featured in three of the four pre-season friendlies. He played against [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].

Ian featured in two consecutive games from Round 1 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 2 ([[Castleford Tigers]]). He missed Rounds 3-15 and Round 4–5 in the [[Challenge Cup]] due to an injury. He featured in two consecutive games from Round 16 ([[Castleford Tigers]]) to Round 17 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]). He was injured for Rounds 18–27. Sibbit announced his retirement from the game on 5 September 2012.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Gordon|first1=James|title=Ian Sibbit announces retirement|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_10132-ian-sibbit-announces-retirement.html|publisher=Love Rugby League|accessdate=11 February 2015|date=6 September 2012}}</ref>
